response.justification: Maria Rodriguez is a strong full-stack and systems engineer with broad polyglot experience, but the role demands deep, current C++ expertise as its core requirement — her only C++ work is a 6-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis project. The overwhelming bulk of her career centers on TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. Equally critical, there is no evidence of industrial automation domain knowledge, IEC 62443 or SDL security practices, MQTT, industrial protocols (OPC UA, Modbus, DNP 3.0), or multi-threaded systems design in C++ — all of which are non-negotiable for this position.
strengths: Demonstrated high-performance, low-latency systems thinking via DLang async I/O work at Dunnhumby handling millions of requests per second
Event-driven and message-broker architecture experience through the GISCollective open-source platform
Node.js and TypeScript proficiency aligns with the role's listed advantageous skills (REST APIs, tooling)
concerns: C++ experience is limited to roughly 6 months (2014–2015) and one thesis project — far below the 8–12 years of active C++ development the role requires
No evidence of industrial automation, IEC 62443 cybersecurity standards, SDL practices, MQTT, or any industrial protocol knowledge
Career trajectory is firmly in web/full-stack engineering; no indication of embedded, on-premises industrial systems, or IoT platform work
